question: Q.1 Select the meaning of: **A bird's eye view**
explanation:
• **A general overview**: A broad look at a situation from a high position. (Related: Panoramic view, Perspective). 🦅
• **A narrow perspective**: Looking at only one small part of a problem. (Antonym: Tunnel vision). 👁️

question: Q.2 If someone is described as **'a cold fish'**, they are:
explanation:
• **Unfriendly**: Someone who seems to lack feelings or personality. (Related: Aloof, Stolid). ❄️
• **Very emotional**: Showing strong feelings easily. (Antonym: Wearing one's heart on one's sleeve). ❤️

question: Q.3 A person who has become **'a laughing stock'** is:
explanation:
• **Ridiculed**: A person who is subjected to general mockery or ridicule. (Related: Butt of the joke). 😂
• **Respected**: Admired by many for achievements. (Antonym: High esteem). 🎖️

question: Q.4 What is a **'left-handed compliment'**?
options: A gift given with the left hand
explanation:
• **Disguised insult**: A remark that seems nice but is actually critical or rude. (Related: Backhanded compliment). ✋
• **High praise**: Strong approval or admiration. (Antonym: Accolade). 🏆

question: Q.5 A **'pipe dream'** refers to:
options: A plan that will definitely succeed
explanation:
• **Impossible hope**: A fantastic but vain hope or plan that is unlikely to happen. (Related: Chasing rainbows). ☁️
• **Plan that will succeed**: A viable and realistic goal. (Antonym: Sure thing). ✅

question: Q.6 Having a **'skeleton in the cupboard'** means having:
explanation:
• **Embarrassing secret**: A shocking or shameful fact that someone wants to keep hidden. (Related: Closet secret). 💀
• **Messy room**: An untidy living space. (Related: Pigsty). 🧹

question: Q.7 A **'wild-goose chase'** is:
explanation:
• **Useless search**: A foolish or hopeless pursuit of something that cannot be found. (Related: Futile, Pointless). 🦢
• **Successful mission**: Achieving the desired result. (Antonym: Mission accomplished). 🎯

question: Q.8 Someone with an **'axe to grind'** has:
options: A lot of work to do
explanation:
• **Selfish reason**: Having a personal motive for doing something or for being involved in a dispute. (Related: Ulterior motive). 🪓
• **No interest**: Being completely impartial or unconcerned. (Antonym: Disinterested). 😐

question: Q.9 To be **'at daggers drawn'** means to be:
explanation:
• **Bitterly hostile**: Two people or groups who are very angry with each other. (Related: Enmity, At loggerheads). 🗡️
• **Very friendly**: Having a cordial or close relationship. (Antonym: Thick as thieves). 🤝

question: Q.10 Being **'at loggersheads'** with someone means:
explanation:
• **Strong disagreement**: Engaged in a serious dispute or quarrel. (Related: At odds). 🪵
• **Working in a team**: Cooperating toward a common goal. (Antonym: In sync). 🤝

question: Q.11 To **'bite the bullet'** means to:
explanation:
• **Endure hardship**: Accepting something difficult or unpleasant because it is unavoidable. (Related: Grin and bear it). 🦷
• **Start a fight**: To initiate a conflict. (Related: Pick a quarrel). ⚔️

question: Q.12 When you **'break the ice'**, you:
options: Start a conversation in a stiff situation
answer: Start a conversation in a stiff situation
explanation:
• **Start conversation**: To do or say something that makes people feel more relaxed. (Related: Breaking the tension). 🧊
• **End a friendship**: Terminating a social relationship. (Antonym: Bury the hatchet). 💔

question: Q.13 **'Burning the candle at both ends'** implies:
options: Working excessively hard and exhausting oneself
answer: Working excessively hard and exhausting oneself
explanation:
• **Exhausting oneself**: To go to bed late and get up early to do a lot of work. (Related: Workaholic). 🕯️
• **Saving energy**: Being frugal with power or effort. (Antonym: Taking it easy). 🛋️

question: Q.14 To **'chew the cud'** means to:
explanation:
• **Ponder deeply**: To think slowly and carefully about something. (Related: Meditate, Ruminate). 🧠
• **Talk loudly**: Speaking with a high volume. (Related: Vociferate). 📢

question: Q.15 **'Crocodile tears'** are:
explanation:
• **Insincere grief**: False tears or a fake show of sorrow. (Related: Hypocrisy, Deception). 🐊
• **Real sorrow**: Genuine sadness or pain. (Antonym: Heartfelt grief). 😢

question: Q.16 To **'cry for the moon'** is to:
explanation:
• **Ask for impossible**: To desire something that is completely out of reach. (Related: Quixotic demand). 🌙
• **Look at stars**: Observing the night sky. (Related: Stargazing). ✨

question: Q.17 If you try to **'curry favour'**, you are:
explanation:
• **Using flattery**: To ingratiate oneself by using praise or attention. (Related: Sycophancy, Apple-polishing). 🍛
• **Being honest**: Acting with integrity and truthfulness. (Antonym: Above board). 📖

question: Q.18 A **'dead ringer'** is someone who:
explanation:
• **Looks exactly like**: A person or thing that is an exact duplicate. (Related: Doppelganger, Spitting image). 👥
• **Is a ghost**: A supernatural spirit. (Related: Phantom). 👻

question: Q.19 To **'eat one's words'** means to:
explanation:
• **Admit wrong**: To retract a statement or admit you were incorrect. (Related: Humble pie). 🥧
• **Keep a promise**: To do what you said you would do. (Antonym: Back out). 🤝

question: Q.20 A **'fly in the ointment'** is:
options: A small problem that spoils things
answer: A small problem that spoils things
explanation:
• **Small problem**: A minor drawback that ruins the enjoyment of something. (Related: Spoiler). 🪰
• **Useful addition**: Something that improves a situation. (Antonym: Asset). 💎

question: Q.21 Having the **'gift of the gab'** means having:
explanation:
• **Great speaking skills**: The ability to speak easily and persuasively. (Related: Eloquence, Articulate). 🗣️
• **Quiet nature**: Being naturally silent or reserved. (Antonym: Taciturn). 🙊

question: Q.22 **'Hard and fast rules'** are:
explanation:
• **Strict**: Rules that are fixed and must be followed without exception. (Related: Rigid, Ironclad). 📏
• **Easy to follow**: Simple or flexible instructions. (Antonym: Lax rules). ➰

question: Q.23 Being **'in the red'** means being:
explanation:
• **In debt**: Owning money or having a negative bank balance. (Related: Overdrawn). 📉
• **Successful**: Achieving wealth or status. (Antonym: In the black/profitable). 📈

question: Q.24 **'Kick the bucket'** is a slang for:
explanation:
• **Dying**: An informal or humorous way to say someone has died. (Related: Passed away). 🪣
• **Starting a journey**: Beginning a trip. (Related: Setting off). ✈️

question: Q.25 The **'Midas touch'** refers to:
options: The ability to succeed in everything
answer: The ability to succeed in everything
explanation:
• **Ability to succeed**: The talent for making money or succeeding in everything one does. (Related: Golden touch). 👑
• **Being greedy**: A strong desire for more than one needs. (Related: Avarice). 🤑

question: Q.26 If a speech is given **'off the cuff'**, it is:
explanation:
• **Spontaneous**: Done without any prior preparation or rehearsal. (Related: Extempore, Impromptu). 🎙️
• **Prepared**: Having planned or studied beforehand. (Antonym: Premeditated). 📝

question: Q.27 If something is **'on the cards'**, it is:
explanation:
• **Likely to happen**: Something that is probable or expected. (Related: Probable, Imminent). 🃏
• **Impossible**: Something that cannot occur. (Antonym: Out of the question). ❌

question: Q.28 **'Red tape'** refers to:
explanation:
• **Excessive bureaucracy**: Formal rules and processes that seem unnecessary and delay results. (Related: Officialdom). 📄
• **Warning sign**: An indicator of danger. (Related: Red flag). 🚩

question: Q.29 Someone who is the **'salt of the earth'** is:
explanation:
• **Honest and good**: A person who is very kind, reliable, and humble. (Related: Noble, Worthy). 🧂
• **Rude**: Lacking manners or being impolite. (Antonym: Churlish). 😠

question: Q.30 To **'turn a deaf ear'** is to:
explanation:
• **Ignore**: To intentionally refuse to listen to or notice something. (Related: Disregard). 👂
• **Listen carefully**: Paying close attention to sounds. (Antonym: All ears). 🔊
